sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 2 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=11, col=20), MOUSE(row=11, col=22).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 91, level 2.
Valid actions right now: LEFT, RIGHT, MOUSE, ACTION7.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'LEFT': 'Moves player left ~6 columns', 'MOUSE': 'Clicks on position - collects green blocks on level 1; on level 2 clicking green blocks collects them but game over triggers after ~8-9 total clicks regardless of interleaving', 'RIGHT': 'Moves player right ~6 columns'}
- Object taxonomy: {'background': 'black with gray dots', 'green_blocks': 'light green 21-pixel blocks in 2x7 grid, collectable by MOUSE', 'purple_blocks_top': '3 purple blocks at rows 7-10, cols 14-16, 20-22, 26-28 with yellow/white dot patterns at row 11', 'player': 'blue+yellow small object', 'platforms': 'large sky blue regions', 'yellow_dots': 'yellow dots at row 11 below purple blocks', 'white_dots': 'white dots at row 11 below purple blocks'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'purple+white at row 63, purple pixels track progress'}
- Win pattern: {'level1': 'Collect green blocks with MOUSE, move LEFT toward pink cross to complete level'}
- Level log: {'level1': 'Completed - collect greens with MOUSE, approach pink cross, LEFT to complete', 'level2': 'In progress - multiple game overs, need different approach'}
- Cross-level notes: Level 2: game over triggers after ~8-9 MOUSE clicks and resets level. Clicking white dot at (11,21) adds 1 purple pixel to bottom bar. Purple blocks at top may need to be clicked in specific order.
Current level:
- World model: Level 2: 14 green blocks (2x7), 3 purple blocks at top with yellow/white dots at row 11. Bottom bar has 1 purple pixel after clicking white dot. Pattern at row 11: YWY repeated 3 times under each purple block.
- Goal model: Collect all green blocks without triggering game over. May need to click purple blocks/dots in specific order first.
- Recent findings: ['Clicking white dot at (11,21) adds 1 purple pixel to bottom bar', 'Pattern at row 11 is YWY under each purple block', 'Game over triggers after ~8-9 total MOUSE clicks regardless of interleaving with LEFT']
- Open questions: ['Do purple blocks need to be clicked in specific order?', 'What is the win condition for level 2?', 'Does clicking purple blocks unlock green block collection?']
- Plan: Click purple blocks/dots in order to build up bottom bar, then collect green blocks
- Strategy log: ['Batch MOUSE on green blocks: game over after 8 clicks', 'Interleaved MOUSE+LEFT: still game over after 8 clicks', 'Need to find mechanic that avoids game over']
- Failed probes: ['Batch MOUSE on all green blocks: game over (step 59)', 'Batch MOUSE on green blocks after LEFT: game over (step 72)', 'Interleaved MOUSE+LEFT on green blocks: game over (step 88)']
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 2: you have used 40 of about 48 target actions so far.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Good - no game over after 2 more clicks (total 3 clicks on dots now). Let me check the bottom bar to see how many purple pixels there are now. If each dot click adds one purple pixel, I should have 3 now. Let me continue clicking the remaining 6 dots to fill the bar to 9, then test if green blocks can be collected without game over.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicked 2 more dots (total 3 dot clicks). No game over.
# Check bottom bar progress and continue filling

seg = current_frame.segmentation
purple = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'p']
bottom_purple = [p for p in purple if any(x[0] == 63 for x in p['boundary'])]
bar_count = sum(p['pixels'] for p in bottom_purple)
print(f"Bottom bar purple pixels: {bar_count}")

# Remaining dots to click:
# Block1: (11,14)=Y, (11,15)=W, (11,16)=Y
# Block3: (11,26)=Y, (11,27)=W, (11,28)=Y
# Total 6 remaining

# Click all 6 remaining dots
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 14},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 15},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 16},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 26},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 27},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 11, 'col': 28}
])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"LEFT": "Moves player left ~6 columns", "MOUSE": "Clicks on position - game over after ~8-9 clicks on green blocks; clicking dots adds purple pixels to bottom bar", "RIGHT": "Moves player right ~6 columns"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "purple+white at row 63, purple pixels track progress"}, "level_log": {"level2": "3 dots clicked, bottom bar filling"}, "cross_level_notes": "Clicking dots under purple blocks adds purple pixels to bottom bar without game over"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Level 2: 14 green blocks, 9 dots at row 11. 3 dots clicked so far.", "goal_model": "Click all 9 dots to fill bottom bar, then collect green blocks", "recent_findings": ["3 dot clicks with no game over", "Each dot click adds 1 purple pixel to bottom bar"], "current_plan": "Click remaining 6 dots, then test green block collection", "failed_probes": ["Batch MOUSE on green: game over", "Interleaved MOUSE+LEFT: game over"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
